report_name: Qilin Ransomware Gang Fuels 30% Surge in Attacks, Heavily Targeting Healthcare

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operator identified as a primary driver of a 30% surge in ransomware attacks in the first half of 2026. The group and its affiliates heavily target the healthcare sector using double-extortion tactics.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: INC Ransom
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as one of the most prolific ransomware operators in the first half of 2026, alongside Qilin.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Qilin affiliates gain initial network access by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing services such as VPNs and RDP.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Used by Qilin affiliates for post-exploitation activities, including reconnaissance and payload execution.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Used by Qilin affiliates to move laterally between systems within a compromised network.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: Qilin affiliates use tools like Mimikatz to harvest credentials from the operating system to escalate privileges.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Before encryption, Qilin affiliates compress and upload large volumes of sensitive data to commercial cloud storage services like Mega.nz or Dropbox.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: In the final stage of an attack, the Qilin ransomware payload is deployed to encrypt critical files across the victim's network, causing operational disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Qilin affiliates may use commands such as 'vssadmin delete shadows' to inhibit system recovery by deleting volume shadow copies.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: Qilin affiliates may conduct brute-force or password-spraying attacks, which can be indicated by a high volume of failed logon events (Windows Event ID 4625).

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: A ransomware payload provided by the Qilin RaaS platform to its affiliates. It is used to encrypt victim data as part of a double-extortion strategy.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Used by Qilin affiliates to perform OS Credential Dumping (T1003) for privilege escalation.

identities.name: Covenant Health
identities.description: A healthcare organization victimized by a Qilin ransomware attack, which resulted in the exposure of 478,188 patient records.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: A primary target for the Qilin ransomware group, with 168 organizations in this sector attacked in the first half of 2026. The high value of patient data makes it a lucrative target.

infrastructure.name: Qilin Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site operated by the Qilin group to publish data stolen from victims who refuse to pay the ransom, enforcing their double-extortion model.

infrastructure.name: Exfiltration Cloud Storage
infrastructure.description: Commercial cloud storage services such as Mega.nz and Dropbox are used by Qilin affiliates to exfiltrate large volumes of victim data before encryption.

indicators.name: Qilin PowerShell Download Cradle
indicators.pattern:
title: Qilin-Affiliated PowerShell Download Cradle
id: 2b9e1c4a-8f7d-4b1e-9c6a-3d2e5f8a7b9c
status: experimental
description: Detects a common PowerShell pattern used to download second-stage malware, as seen in Qilin affiliate activity.
references:
  - https://thecyberexpress.com/qilin-inc-ransom-drive-2026-ransomware-surge/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-03
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString'
      - 'IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell being used to download second-stage malware, a technique associated with Qilin affiliates.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound Transfer to Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Outbound Transfer to Cloud Storage
id: 5d8c3a9b-1e4f-4a7b-8d6c-9f0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects large outbound data transfers to common cloud storage providers, which may indicate data exfiltration by groups like Qilin.
references:
  - https://thecyberexpress.com/qilin-inc-ransom-drive-2026-ransomware-surge/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-03
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'dropbox.com'
  # This rule would ideally be a correlation rule checking for bytes_out > threshold, but as an atomic rule, this is a starting point.
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to known commercial cloud storage providers, a TTP used by Qilin for data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Anomalous LSASS Process Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous LSASS Process Access for Credential Dumping
id: 9e1c4a8f-7d4b-4e1b-9c6a-3d2e5f8a7b9d
status: experimental
description: Detects non-system processes accessing the LSASS process memory, a common technique for credential dumping used by tools like Mimikatz.
references:
  - https://thecyberexpress.com/qilin-inc-ransom-drive-2026-ransomware-surge/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-03
tags:
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_access
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetImage|endswith: '\lsass.exe'
  filter:
    SourceImage|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects suspicious access to the LSASS process memory, indicative of credential dumping activities.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 1c4a8f7d-4b1e-4b9c-9c6a-3d2e5f8a7b9e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://thecyberexpress.com/qilin-inc-ransom-drive-2026-ransomware-surge/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-03
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies, a technique used to inhibit system recovery.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Outbound Transfer to Cloud Storage
